carl_floor1:
“Mordecai, darling. Is there a worst-dressed award for the dungeon?”[1]

This page documents Carl's gear, levels, stats, and skills as he progresses through the First Floor.

Gear
Starting Gear

Carl enters the Dungeon with only his leather coat, boxers, Beatrice's ill-fitting pink croc sandals, a Zippo lighter, and half a pack of Marlboro Red cigarettes.[2][3]

Gear Gained

Within an hour of entering the First Floor, Carl receives a Goblin Pass Tattoo on his left inner forearm, which he grumbles about because he had never wanted tattoos.[4][5] At the same time, he puts on the Enchanted Trollskin Shirt of Pummeling, attaches the Enchanted Nightgaunt Cloak of Stoutness to his leather coat (Mordecai later notes that it occupies his "neck" slot), and puts the Enchanted Toe Ring of the Splatter Skunk onto his right index toe.[5][6] Some six hours later, he gains a Silver Ring of +1 CON, which he wears on a finger.[7]

He wears unenchanted fingerless gloves for the first two days or so, and wraps a chainlink leash around his right fist during the Hoarder battle (around one day after the Dungeon opens).[8] After the battle, he receives and immediately equips the Enchanted Spiked Kneepads of the Shade Gnoll Riot Forces.[9] With 3 Days, 2 Hours to level collapse, he punches a Slime, which dissolves his right glove and the right arm of his leather jacket, but leaves the chain on his fist gleaming and polished.[10]

Carl gains the Goblin Copper Chopper. With 2 Days 18 Hours to level collapse, Carl receives the Desperado Pass Tattoo on his neck and a Silver Ring of +2 CON, which he wears on his left ring finger.[11] With just under 2 days to level collapse, he receives the Enchanted War Gauntlet of the Exalted Grull and equips it on his right wrist.[12]

Spells

Carl does not learn any spells on the First Floor.

Passive Effects
Attacks Gain
2% chance to stun target when struck with Gauntlet.
All Equipped Armor gains:
Anti-Piercing Resistance (from Nightgaunt Cloak).
10% Damage Reflect (from Spiked Kneepads).
Negated Effects/Attacks:
Melee-based Damage Debuffs (from Trollskin Shirt).
Momentum-based attacks (from Kneepads).
Resistant to:
Ice attacks (from Nightgaunt Cloak).
Poison attacks (from Nightgaunt Cloak).
Weak to:
Goblin enemies deal +20% damage to Carl (from Goblin Pass Tattoo).
Memberships & Allies
Desperado Club membership (from Desperado Pass Tattoo)
Goblins are white-tagged; free passage in goblin territory (from Goblin Pass).
Other
By the end of the floor, he can barely feel the soles of his feet. The sides of his feet are still sensitive.[15]
Ratings

Ratings are not active on the First Floor.

References
↑ Dinniman, Matt. Dungeon Crawler Carl (Chapter 12) (p. 108). Dandy House. Kindle Edition.

donut_floor1:
This page documents Princess Donut's levels, stats, skills, and gear on the First Floor (5 Days).

Carl	Floor 1 • 2 • 3 • 4 • 5 • 6 • 7 • 8 • 9
Donut	Floor 1 • 2 • 3 • 4 • 5 • 6 • 7 • 8 • 9
Jump To Section	Gear • Levels & Stats • Skills • Spells • Passive Effects • Ratings
Contents
1	Gear
1.1	Starting Gear
1.2	Gained Gear
1.3	Ending Gear
2	Levels & Stats
3	Skills
4	Spells
5	Passive Effects
6	References
Gear
Starting Gear

Donut enters the Dungeon wearing a collar and nothing else.

Gained Gear

Within one hour, she grows slightly larger as a result of her transformation from the Enhanced Pet Biscuit and equips the Enchanted Crown of the Sepsis Whore.[1] Just under seven hours later, she receives and equips the Talisman of the Slate Butterfly.[2]

With two days and 18 hours to level collapse, she receives a Desperado Pass Tattoo just above her right shoulder blade; it glows through her fur, but looks like nothing so much as a gold-colored splotch. At the same time, she gains and equips the Enchanted Fae Scale Quadruped Crupper of the Fleet.[3]

With less than 40 hours remaining in the Dungeon, she gains a Bracelet of +2 DEX, which she equips on her front leg.[4]
